activities_projects.title: Magnesium Ribbon Observation
activities_projects.objective: Observe a combination reaction and the formation of magnesium oxide
steps: Clean a small piece of magnesium ribbon with sandpaper to remove the oxide layer
Hold it with a pair of tongs and heat it over a burner
Collect the white ash formed in a watch glass
Note the type of flame produced during burning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that burning magnesium in air is a chemical change resulting in a new substance (MgO)
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct description of the dazzling white flame and the nature of the ash formed (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Lead Nitrate and Potassium Iodide Reaction
activities_projects.objective: Observe a double displacement reaction and precipitation
steps: Take lead nitrate solution in one test tube
Add potassium iodide solution to it
Observe the immediate change in color
Record if any solid substance settles at the bottom
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the formation of a precipitate as a sign of a chemical reaction
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of the yellow precipitate (Lead Iodide) and the reaction type (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Zinc Granules and Dilute Acid
activities_projects.objective: Observe the evolution of gas during a metal-acid reaction
materials: Dilute hydrochloric acid or sulphuric acid
steps: Place a few zinc granules in a conical flask
Add dilute acid carefully
Observe the bubbles forming around the zinc granules
Test the gas by bringing a burning matchstick near the mouth of the flask
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that the evolution of hydrogen gas is a characteristic of certain chemical reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate reporting of the 'pop' sound test for hydrogen gas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Change in Temperature Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between exothermic and endothermic processes
steps: Take a small amount of calcium oxide in a beaker
Slowly add water to it
Touch the beaker and note the sensation
Record the temperature change if a thermometer is available
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies exothermic reactions where heat is released
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why the beaker becomes hot and writing the chemical equation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Iron Nails in Copper Sulphate
steps: Take blue-colored copper sulphate solution in a test tube
Clean iron nails with sandpaper and immerse them in the solution using a thread
Leave them undisturbed for 20 minutes
Compare the color of the solution and the nail with the original samples
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how a more reactive metal displaces a less reactive metal
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct observation of the color change from blue to light green (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chemical Equation Balancing Act
activities_projects.objective: Practice the Hit-and-Trial method of balancing equations
steps: Write down 5 unbalanced skeletal equations (e.g., Fe + H2O → Fe3O4 + H2)
Count the atoms of each element on the reactant and product sides
Apply coefficients to equalize the number of atoms
Verify the final balanced equation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies the Law of Conservation of Mass to chemical reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the 5 balanced equations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Types of Reactions Sorting Game
activities_projects.objective: Categorize reactions based on their characteristics
steps: Create cards with different chemical equations
Create category cards: Combination, Decomposition, Displacement, Double Displacement
Sort the equation cards under the correct category
Explain the logic for each placement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between the four main types of chemical reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification of at least 8 out of 10 equations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Corrosion and Rancidity Poster
activities_projects.objective: Understand the effects of oxidation in daily life
materials: Pictures of rusted iron or spoiled food
steps: Define corrosion and rancidity on the poster
List three ways to prevent rusting (e.g., painting, galvanizing)
Explain why chips packets are flushed with nitrogen gas
Draw or paste examples of these phenomena
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates chemical oxidation to real-world material degradation and food spoilage
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of prevention methods and visual presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Decomposition of Lead Nitrate
steps: Take a small amount of lead nitrate in a dry boiling tube
Heat the boiling tube over a flame
Observe the color of the fumes emitted
Note the change in the color of the residue
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies thermal decomposition and the evolution of nitrogen dioxide gas
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of brown fumes (NO2) and the remaining yellow residue (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Invisible Ink (Acid-Base Reaction)
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate a chemical change using indicators
steps: Write a secret message on paper using a cotton swab dipped in lemon juice
Let it dry completely until invisible
Heat the paper gently over a bulb or wipe it with a baking soda solution
Observe the message reappearing
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that color changes indicate chemical transformations
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the chemical reaction that made the writing visible (10 marks)
